

=== LLD (Low Level Design) ===


--- What is LLD and Pattern Categories? Difference between Is-a & Has-a relationship ---

--- SOLID Principles ---

Another side.
Welcome back to the channel.
And today in LD we will see solid principles.
So before I start with this topic I will tell you why it is required.
So see we we know that there are so many design patterns we have and each pattern follow this principle.
And if you remember, I also told you that the usage of this pattern is not mandatory.
Let's say in interview you got certain question, okay that hey design parking lot or design XYZ any
system.
So it is not mandatory that you should use certain pattern.
You can come up with the classes, objects, their relationship by your own.
Okay, that's totally fine.
But whatever the solution, whatever the classes and relationship which you are coming up.
Okay.
That should follow these principles.
So that's what I am trying to say, that these principles are the guideline.
You can say that these are the fundamentals.
No matter if you are using a pattern, that pattern, we know that it's a already tested it's a flexible
solution to a already.
You can say that some problem which already exists.
So we don't have to reinvent the wheel in identifying the same or solving the same problem.
Again, we can directly use the pattern, but we know that those patterns which are present, they are
already following those principles.
And these principles are the one that actually make this design clean, flexible, okay and easy to
maintain.
So again, usage of pattern is not mandatory.
You can come up with your completely new design of your classes and objects.
But these design whichever you are coming up with should follow this principle.
Now let's see what are these principle?
So we have solid which stands for S single responsibility O open closed principle L Liskov substitution
principle I interface segregation principle.
Dependency inversion principle okay.
And this advantage which gives us is that help us to write better code.
Avoid duplicate code.
Easy to maintain.
Easy to understand flexible reduce complexity.
So if you follow this principle these all advantages we can get.
Now let's see each principle one by one.
What is single responsibility principle.
It states that a class should have only one reason to change, meaning it should have only one responsibility,
one job.
And I will also tell you there is one confusion which engineers get that, uh, I will tell you after
this.
After once I told you the solution and the solution, then I will tell you that what generally question
engineers ask.
Okay, so with single responsibility, we get to know that what definition says that a class should
have only one responsibility?
Okay.
So understand with this example which violates the single responsibility.
So this is one object as an object marker which we are using it okay.
And now we sorry.
So I have an invoice class.
This invoice class is responsible for doing certain computation okay.
So this is let's say a business logic to compute the total price.
It is also responsible for saving the data to DB.
And also it is responsible for printing the invoice.
So this class is responsible for so many things.
Now the problem with the above code is that In future, let's say you have to change the logic of insertion
into the DB.
So let's say save to db.
This is SQL.
Now in future you want to change it to NoSQL.
You have to change the code.
Then let's say today you are printing the invoice.
Now you have to future you have to change the logic.
Then you again have to change this class.
So for each, when there are more responsibilities, then it is possible that this already class which
is tested and in production and with every change it has to be tested again.
So there is a higher chance of human error.
Okay.
So this violates SRP because if the tax calculation rule change in future we need to modify the invoice
class.
If database structure change again we have to modify this class.
So if there are so many requirements we are putting, then there is a high chance that this class will
get modified too frequently.
So here, if you are following the single responsibility, let's see how it can be.
You can improve.
This code can be improved.
Again, this is an object.
Same thing.
Nothing new in that one.

Okay, so now I will tell you what.
One question generally comes from an engineer that hey, does it mean that one class can only have one

No.
What I mean to say here is that one class should have only one responsibilities.
You can say that the logical grouping.
Ping.
If we are going to keep only one method in one class, we are going to have like class explosion.
We have to create so many classes.
It just like logical grouping we are doing so all methods which are related to computation we can put
into this class.
There could be 1020 methods which is related to DB.

So you can reuse it also because it's in a separate class.
The second is open closed principle.
So open closed principle says that a class should be open for extension but closed for modification.
What does it mean?
It means that new functionality can be added through inheritance rather than modifying existing code.
The problem with that is that existing code is already tested and deployed in production.
Adding modification would introduce additional risk and required additional testing effort.
So understand with this example.
So yes we have created invoice Dao right.
So it is responsible for saving into the DB Okay, it's already tested and it's already live.
Now let's say that in future I have added one more that save to file.
Earlier it was saved to DB.
I have added save to file in future.
Let's say this one is SQL.
I have added another class save to NoSQL.
So another thing comes is that.
You have to keep modify the class which is already tested.
Okay.
And why you have to modify because you are adding a new feature.
So save to DB it was there.
Then save to file.
It's a new feature.
Then you are modifying the existing class then save to NoSQL DB.
This is again a new feature, but again you are modifying the existing class.
So this violating this principle what it says that open for extension but closed for modification.
So we achieve this with inheritance.
So we can create invoice Dao interface and we can have multiple child.
Okay.
One is DB invoice Dao one is for DB specific logic.
Another is file specific feature.
Another is let's say in memory.
Or then you have future let's say NoSQL depend.
So whenever new feature is there you can extend it.
Okay.
So it's like open for extension okay.
It is open.
But let's say in future you are adding NoSQL DB support.
So you will add a new child class.
You don't need to modify the existing one which are already tested and in life.
So that's what we did.
So invoice Dao now is an interface.
So database invoice Dao implements this interface and it override this save method and provide its implementation.
Similarly for file invoice.
Now it does the same thing.
Okay.
So it implements the invoice and provide an implementation for it.
Safe.
How it wanted to save the data.

But the intention is different.
So LSP principle more focuses on that from the client side code.
The functionality we should not decrease it.
Okay.
So let's say you have an interface and you have multiple child classes.
It's more focus is on that.
They provide some consistent behavior.
Okay.
This behavior should be present.

So LSP principle is more on to that.
And it is more focused on that.
That okay.
If any object don't have consistent behavior take it out and put it into different interface.
Okay.
And it's more focus on that.
Your client code should not break when you switch between these objects okay.
So object one.
Object two.
Object three.
So even if you switch between this objects which are part of the same interface, your code should not
break.
Yeah.

Functionalities that the contract and LSP is more focused on that.
Okay.
If something is not, uh, providing certain behavior, which is the contract here, then take it out,
okay.
And here in the interface, segregation is more on that.
Like the child class, we should not force it that, hey, provide an implementation of it.
And we know that it's going to never, never have this feature and it's still unnecessary.

So it more focusing on the interface.
Create a cleaner interfaces.
Don't blot it with so many operations okay I hope that is clear.
And the last one is the dependency inversion principle.
So dependency inversion principle states that high level component should not depend on low level component
directly.
Instead they should depend on abstraction considered with this example.
So here let's say I have an interface keyboard and I have two child classes.
Wired keyboard.
Again wired keyboard is a keyboard, and Bluetooth keyboard is a keyboard.
So these are two children.
Now see this example which is violating the dependency inversion principle.
So I have an object map book.
Now here if you see I am depending.
So MacBook has via keyboard.
So now it is totally depend on low level module directly on this wired keyboard.
So in future if you want to support Bluetooth keyboard would you be able to do it dynamically?
No.
You have to change this class.
So that's what dependency inversion says that hey, you should rely on abstraction rather than low level
components.
And how you can do that.
See that one now in the MacBook I depend on keyboard.
Keyboard is an interface, so it can hold an object of wire keyboard.
It can hold an object of Bluetooth keyboard.
So when creating an object, we can decide what we want it.
So now let's say that first time I am creating an object MacBook and I provided a keyboard.
So now MacBook has wire keyboard.
Now next time I'm creating a MacBook I provided the Bluetooth keyboard object.
So see that is also supported.
But previously when we have hard coded the wired keyboard, is it possible to supply the Bluetooth keyboard?
No, you have already hard coded wire.
There is no way you can dynamically change it.
So that's why we have to rely on abstraction interfaces majorly.
Okay, so I hope this solid principles are clear.

--- Strategy Design Pattern (Behavioral Pattern) ---

Back to the channel.
And today we will see strategy design pattern.
So it's a behavioral design pattern.
And we know that behavioral is like more interested in how objects interact or communicate with each
other okay.
And it defines multiple algorithms, encapsulates their logic in dedicated class and enable changing
an algorithms behavior at runtime.
What does that mean?
So understand with this problem and solution.
And when we will read this definition again it will get more clear.
So see this problem.
First I have a class vehicle and it has a default drive capability.
Let's say normal drive capability.
Then I have multiple child class like sports vehicle off road vehicle passenger vehicles.
Or you can have further like goods vehicle.
So many child classes.
Now each child class can have their own drive functionality.
Either they can have the same default or they can change the drive capability what they want.
let's say sport, we can have sport drive capability.
Off road vehicle also has sport drive functionality.
Passenger is reusing the parent one normal drive functionality.
Okay, so one thing, one problem we can easily identify is that that there are so many child classes,
it is possible that there is a code duplication.
So in this case sports vehicle also need sports drive functionality.
Off road also needs sports drive functionality.
Okay.
So this is one problem.
Code duplication is there.
So that is one problem.
Code duplication.
Same sport drive functionality present into many sub classes.
And this is just one example.
So consider that you have 1015 sub classes.
So there is high chance that there is a code duplication.
And the second is tight coupling.
If in future we need to change the drive functionality to say off road vehicle, it is not possible
without updating the class.
Now let's say that today you have a sports functionality for this a sport vehicle.
In future, if you have to change this capability, would you be able to change it?
No.
You have already defined this sports capability here.
Similarly here for passenger vehicle, is it possible for you to change the behavior like once you define
it?
No.
You have to change the code.
So we have tight coupling here also.
So using a strategy pattern we can solve both.
So this is the UML diagram for it okay.
So here if you see the UML diagram we create a strategy.
And then we have a multiple concrete implementation.
So this strategy class or interface is nothing but like the algorithm here in the above problem.
What is the algorithm here.
Like this one.
This drive capability.
So some has normal drive capability.
Some has the sports drive capability like.
So I have created this strategy drive strategy as a separate interface.
and it has one abstract method drive.
Now we have multiple normal drive.
Its post drive.
And even in future if we support more, we can have that also.
And in the normal drive, yes, we have a complete functionality or algorithm for the normal drive capability
in the drive.
In this drive method we have the complete code for the sports drive functionality.
So this part is done.
Okay now we have a context.
So here in this context is our vehicle.
So now in this vehicle if you see that we are accepting the drive strategy dynamically.
So first of all how many different child classes of the vehicle has let's say I have a good vehicle.
I have an off road road vehicle, I have a sports vehicle.
Or let's say we can have more, let's say passenger vehicle.
So we have different child classes of this vehicle.
Now if you see one very important thing is that this child class Is telling what drive strategy they
need.
So when you are creating an object of goods vehicle, you can pass that whatever the drive strategy
you need, whether you want normal drive strategy or sports drive strategy or what.
Okay.
So it's dynamic now.
Okay.
So let's say today goods vehicle has normal drive strategy.
And in future you can change it.
You can when creating an object you can pass a sports drive.
That is also possible.
Similarly for off road, whenever you create an object of off road vehicle we can provide what's the
drive strategy we want whether we want sports drive, normal drive or some something else.
Okay.
And in future we can change it whatever we want and whatever the drive strategy you have, you just
call the drive method and that functionality will work.
So here, if you see the code, I created a new sports vehicle.
I am creating a sports vehicle and while creating an object I pass.
What is the strategy I wanted?
I want sports drive feature, I want this.
Okay, so now I created an object.
And once you have created the object when you call the drive okay.
So this is the default method when you call the drive, whatever the strategy you have chosen that drive
functionality would be invoked.
So here if you see that what we have done is we have separate out the algorithms okay.
And now because of that, it is possible that we can extend the new capability into the drive strategy
okay.
So independently it can scale.
And similarly independently we can scale the vehicles.
Also we can add more vehicles.
And also dynamically we can manage the strategy.
So we have removed the tight coupling.
And also we have removed the code duplication also.

--- Observer Pattern (Behavioral Pattern) ---

Side.
Welcome back to the channel.
And today we will see Observer Pattern.
So let's start without wasting any time.
So it's a behavioral design pattern where an object which is also known as observable.
Or you can say that publisher maintains a list of dependent say observers.
Okay.
We will get more clear about this term uh, soon.
And the main purpose of it is to automatically notify the dependents observer whenever there is a change
in its state.
In which state in.

or all the dependents who are observing it.

This is just a heads up.
Do whatever now you want to do.

station.

the weather station.

--- Decorator Design Pattern (Structural Design Pattern) ---

This site.
Welcome back to the channel.
And today we are going to do Decorator pattern.
So let's understand the use case first so that it would be clear in what all use cases we might have
to apply decorator pattern.
So in this example if you see this core base object.
So whatever the base object you have for example our base pizza like a margherita pizza.
Right.
So these are the core.
And on top of that base object we can add feature okay.
So now here if you that I have added whatever the object base object I have, I have decorated with
feature one, you have added new feature okay.
So similarly now here if you see in this example the base pizza which is Margherita, you can add another
feature of it like cheese.
You decorated it with cheese okay.
Then again you can repeat it.
Whatever the result which you have got.
Again you can add another feature on top of it, another feature on top of it, and it goes on.
Okay, so it has a stacking feature.
Okay.
Or sometimes also known as layering effect.
Okay.
So the same example let's say you have a base pizza margherita.
You add another feature on top of it.
You added cheese.
And on top of that you added another feature, let's say mushroom.
Okay.
So you have now Margherita pizza.
Then you have added cheese on top of it, then you added mushroom on top of it.
And similarly you have added another feature.
Let's say you have added it another paneer and goes on.

So in simple term, whenever you find a use case where you have a layering effect or stacking feature,
okay, that base object, then you have feature one plus feature two plus feature three.
Likewise, then you should know that it's a hint for applying the decorator pattern.

on.

--- Factory & Abstract Factory pattern (Creational Design Pattern) ---

Share on this site.
Welcome back.
And today in low level design we are going to cover factory pattern and abstract factory pattern.
Okay so let's understand the problem first.
So let's say that this is our product shape.
It's an interface.
And we have multiple child classes like circle square right.
So circle is a shape a square is a shape.
And we have multiple classes in our project class A, class B, class C, class D, class C.
And they are using this product.
So they are creating a new object.
Okay.
So let's say class A is creating new circle.
Class B is creating new square.
So like this the object creation is used.
Or you can say that it is spread across our whole repository.
Okay.
So the problem with this is that in future let's say the instance Stan's creation.
Logic for circle is change.
Say we need to pass now.
New circle and the radius.
We need to pass the radius.
New circle and you have to pass the radius.
Now all the classes which is doing new circle.
New circle right everywhere we have to make a change.
So this is becoming too complex and difficult to manage.
So that's where our factory pattern comes into the picture which is the creational design pattern.
And it controls the creation of an object like so it used when we want to encapsulate object creation
and related creation logic at one place.
So if we want to control the object creation and we want to hold it, encapsulate it only at one place,
that's where the factory pattern comes into the picture.
So many times when we are learning, we get confused because there are two flavors of it simple factory
pattern and factory method pattern.
This is something which is there in the books, okay, and simple factory pattern is something which
is you might see in industry.
So when you are working in an in your company, if you see the code, you will see that many people
or many engineers are following simple factory pattern.
But if you read the books you will see factory method pattern.
So it creates contradiction like which one is right?
Which one is wrong.
So generally both are right.
But I would say that this is in the books, which is factory method pattern.
But this is also not wrong.
It's a highly used simple factory pattern is also used a lot in industry, but it is nowhere like uh,
official.
Okay, so let's first see simple factory pattern.
So we have our product shape and it has certain method.
And we have our child class like circle which is a shape.
Square is a shape.
So it's a child class of shape.
So circle is just providing an implementation for these abstract method.
Very simple.
Nothing special in this.
So only thing is like we have a parent and we have a child.
So this is let's say our product.
Okay.
Now in the simple factory we have one class factory class shape factory.
In the shape factory we create one method which is static.
Okay.
So so that it can be invoked with the class itself.
Because at this point of time, we haven't created any object and we want an object of a particular
shape.
So we have created a shape factory inside that we have create shape method, which is static so that
it can be invoked through a class itself.
Now if you see inside it, it has certain if else if condition, if whatever the shape type you wanted,
it could be a string or let's say here.
In this case it's an enum circle square.
But let's say if it is a string also.
So if shape type which you want is circle, then it returns a new circle object.
If the shape type which you wanted is square, then it will return a new square object.
So now everywhere, whatever the code, let's say class A, so now it will not do new circle directly.
It will invoke this factory like this.
It will invoke the factory shape.
Factory dot create shape.
Hey, give me an object of circle and it will give you an instance.
So now the logic of.
or creation of an object holds only at one place in future.
Any change is happening only happened at one place.
We don't have to touch different classes in a repo.
But there is one disadvantage.
Disadvantage is that it violates open closed principle.
If any new shape is introduced, then we have to touch this factory class.
Yeah.
So let's say that rectangle class is introduced.
So here in this case we have a new rectangle class.
New rectangle class.
So we have to update this factory method also.
So inside this we already have circle.
We already have a square.
Now we have to put else if if the shape if you want it is rectangle.
Create an object of rectangle.
So it violates open closed principle that whenever any new shape is introduced this method will also
need to be changed.
Okay.
Another thing is that this factory class can become bloated over time.
Say, if object creation logic is complex, it is not simple as new circle.
It is complex.
Say in this example, if shape type equals to equals to circle, this is just for example.
So I am just loading a configuration.
I am validating the configuration.
I am initializing some resources in db.
Let's say something something and then creating a circle object.
Just assume that it's a lot of complex process to create an object.
So what would happen is your this class become bloated very difficult to manage okay.
And also it violates single responsibility principle.
Why?
Because now this class is doing two things.
First is selection okay.
Based on the shape type it need to select okay.
Whether I have to create a circle object or a square object.
After that, it also has construction logic.
All the logics present inside it is also controlled or managed by it.
So in future if rectangle comes again this class change, or if construction logic change, let's say
now you don't need to validate.
Then again this class change.
So it is doing two things now.
So it's also valid single responsibility principle.
So this simple factory pattern is used when your use case is very simple.
Okay.
And that's where next is factory method pattern comes into the picture.
So understand this factory method pattern is that we have a shape circle square.
This is our product.
No nothing different in that.
Okay.

One factory for one product okay.

You understood that.
So this is factory method pattern where for each product we have a respective factory.

Now here if you see that advantage is same so how we will invoke it.

Also another thing is it solves the bloating issue and it also solves single responsibility violation.

Square.

--- Design Tic-Tac-Toe Game ---

They said welcome back.
And today we are going to do low level design of tic tac toe.
If you are experienced or fresher, this is the most simplest level which we can get in an interview.
Okay, so let's start without wasting any time.
So let's understand about the game and its rules.
So tic tac toe we all we have played a lot right.
So it's a generally a three by three.
We have a matrix and it's a two player game.
And one player.
We assign a symbol let's say cross and another we have assign let's say circle.
So in this one the we each player takes a turn one by one and places their symbol onto this grid three
by three grid.
And the winner is whichever player got their symbol either on a full row or column or diagonal.
Okay, so that's what I have written here.
It's a simple two player paper and pencil game.
We have all played it.
Each player select a piece before the game player takes turn, placing it on the three by three grid.
A player wins by placing three of its pieces in a row, column, or diagonals.
Okay, and the game ends when a player wins or when all the grid cells are filled.

--- Chain Of Responsibility Design Pattern (Behavioral Design Pattern) ---

This site.
Welcome back to the channel.
And today we are going to do the chain of responsibility pattern which is a behavioral design.
Pattern means focus on how to objects communicate with each other okay.
So in this a request is passed through along a chain of handlers okay.
Each handler decide either to process the request or pass it to the next handler in the chain.
Understand this with this real life example, like you might be working in a company.
Okay, so where we have multiple you can say that locks level.
We have debug log level.
We have info log level, we have error log level.
And similarly we in future we can increase the chain.
We have a fatal log level.
Right.
And whenever a request let's say we want to log, we just say that we start from the first chain.
Understand this line that with this we pass the request to a chain of handler.
We just pass it to this chain.
Hey, then this will check.
Hey, can I handle this request or can I handle this log request?
If yes, then it will handle it and return it from here.
If no, then it will pass to the next handler in the chain.
Okay, then it will check.
Hey, can I handle this log request?
If yes, then no further processing will happen.
It will just process it and return it from here.
Otherwise it will pass the request to the next handler in the chain.
That's where it says that.
So it's a pattern that passes request along a chain of handler.
Each handler decides either to process or pass it to the next handler in the chain.
Okay.
And yeah, one real life example is you can say that atm.
So in this let's say understand the ATM example, real world would be let's say user need to withdraw,
let's say ₹50 okay.
And you have different handler, let's say ₹20 handler, ₹50 handler.
And then you have, let's say ₹100 handler.
Okay.
So now let's say that a request goes here.
Let's say user need to withdraw ₹50.

50.

--- Proxy Design Pattern (Structural Pattern) ---

Hey guys.
Welcome back to the channel.
And today we will do proxy design pattern.
So it's a part of a structural category okay.
So it provides a representative or placeholder for another object to control access to it.
So understand this with a simple diagram.
So here we have a client which is trying to access this real object.
So proxy as the name it is placing in mid between this client and real object.
And it controls the access okay.
So client is never directly talking with the real objects.
So it all the communication is happen through proxy okay.
So use cases of proxy design patterns are like if you are working with the spring boot or if your day
to day like everywhere, you can find that yes, there is a proxy involved in that.
So first very useful use cases.
Access control.
So it restricts access to sensitive sensitive operations based on user permission.
So let's say a client is trying to perform a delete operation.
Okay.
So instead of directly calling the delete we can have a proxy where it will check the permission.
Okay.
If user has permission then only let the user invoke the delete method.
Otherwise block the request itself.
Okay.
So this is one use case of proxy.
Another important use case of proxy is pre and post processing.
So if you are from spring boot background or normal even if not spring boot background normal.
If you are working at a back end project, you know that when we are trying to update the DB internally,
we have to create a transaction.
We have to start the transaction and then perform a DB operation, and then we have to end or roll back
the transaction based upon its success.
Okay.
So generally in spring boot example I am giving when we use at the rate transaction annotation, we
just perform db operation.
Hey this is the DB operation which I want to do and you just annotate it with at the annotation.
Internally it make use of proxy design pattern.
So it does some pre-processing and post-processing.
So it intercept.
So instead of directly calling the DB operations there is a proxy class got invoked first which start
the transaction which is nothing but a pre-processing.
So it does some additional work before the actual task.
So to start the transaction then whatever the DB operation you wanted to perform, it does that.
After that the post-processing if the operation is success and the transaction else or and the commit
the transaction else rollback the transaction.
So this is a very very good example for you can say that pre and post processing use case of a proxy
design pattern.
Okay.
So this definition is clear.
And the use cases are clear.
Like what exactly proxy design pattern is.
So it's very very easy to understand with the class diagram.
And then we will see an example.
So it would be proper clear.
So we have an interface say subject.
And we have a real implementation of it.
So this is a concrete class okay.
So consider this is a real object.
And client is interested in accessing the real object.
But here in the proxy design pattern we also create one more child class of this subject which is a
proxy proxy subject okay.
It is also a concrete class.
Now the very important part is that this proxy has a reference of this real subject.
Now why it has?
Because now the client will never talk directly with the real subject.
It talk with the proxy, and the proxy need a reference so that it can further invoke the real subject.
So understand with this example.
So here I have created an employee Dao.
So this employee employee Dao has two method get employee info and create employee.
So two method get and create.
And this is the real the actual object which performs the thing.
So it is a child of this employee Dao get it is fetching the data from the DB.
This is create creation of an employee in the DB.
Okay, now here if you see I have created one proxy employee Dao proxy.
And here I have this reference.
So whenever the employee Dow proxy object is going to get constructed.
So this is a constructor.
So I am creating a reference of this one actual object.
So it has the reference of this employee Dow impl.
The real object.
Okay.
So this got.
Created instance is filled up whenever this constructor is invoked.
Okay.
And it has certain fields which this proxy is required to do its validation.
So let's say currently I'm just using client role.
So based on that client role it will decide certain rules.
Let's say that now these two methods it will provide an implementation.
Get employee info and create employee.
So get employee info.
So now here first it is checking that if client role whatever the client role we have is equals to admin
or client role is user, then it will allow to access the request.
So whatever the object.
Now I have for the actual object, it will use it and call the method.
So now this will get invoked properly.
Else it will throw an exception.
Access denied.
So this is I'm showcasing the use case one.
Whatever we have discussed this one.
Access control.
Okay.
Similarly the other method implementation is.
So here if you understand that what proxy is doing is proxy is just controlling the access here.
Hey, should I provided access or not?
Implementation is still there into the actual method okay.
It just do certain validation and then forward the request to the actual object okay.
Similarly for create employee it just check whatever the client role we have.
Is it admin.
Then only let the create employee method request pass through.
Otherwise Access denied.
Okay, so here I am creating a proxy object.
So now this is my client.
So here the client is trying to access object, but not through real subject.
Through proxy.
So this is my client.
Now first client is creating an object of employed proxy and passes the role user.
So here in this constructor it passes the client role as user.
So currently the role is assigned as user.
And in the constructor new object of this employee Dao impl would be created.
So this is done at this one.
So now this proxy object I am calling first employee info.
So it will say that okay whatever the role is admin or user.
Yes it is user.
So it will allow.
Now I am with the same object I am trying to call create employee.
So create employee has admin but minus user.
Okay I created this object with the user role.
It won't be able to access it.
So it would say access denied.
Very very simple okay.
But highly used.
It might not be like in your day to day work.
You might not see it, but it is very heavily used when you will start working with Spring Boot framework.
You realize that, or even not with just Spring Boot, even with JUnit, even with Spring Boot, and
within Java itself, like we have annotation reflection internally proxy is involved.
Okay, so before our real object got invoked, this proxy, some layer catches it, perform some additional
work, and then ultimately pass the object pass to the real object.
Okay.
Let me know if there is any doubt we can discuss further into the comment section.
Okay guys.
Thank you.
Bye.

--- Null Object Design Pattern (Behavioral Pattern) ---

Said, welcome back to the channel.
And today we will do null object pattern which belongs to a category of behavioral pattern okay.
So let's first understand the problem before even we try to understand what does null object pattern
does.
Okay.
So let's say that the problem is the our code without null check lead to an unexpected behavior due
to null pointer exception.
We all know that we are working in an industry, and we know that null pointer exception is the biggest.
You can say that error contributor sometime okay, resulting in a poor user experience.
This is also known as.
This is also a bad programming practice because null pointer exception in a code means it's a server
error and it is not handled properly and it is not expected.
Okay.
So this shows a use case which is not handled properly.
And that's why it's a bad programming practice.
And that's one reason we have to put so many not equals to null checks everywhere across the code,
so that we can avoid null pointer exception.
I have seen engineers even with every line of code, they feel like okay, if I put a null pointer exception
if not equals to null check even though it is not necessary, but they just add it.
Just it gives a little confidence that okay null pointer exception should not come.
That's why like we as an engineer we all know that we fear this null pointer exceptions a lot because
if this comes in life it is does not look good.
And that's where we use lot of not equals to null checks across the project which makes it difficult
to manage.
And it is also a polluting the code unnecessarily.
So understand this with an example.
So here I have a vehicle interface which has very two simple uh, you can say that no need to put abstract
internally, it automatically does that.
We have a start and stop method.
Okay so I have two class car which is a vehicle.
So it's a child of this vehicle class.
Similarly bike it is a vehicle.
So it is also a child of this vehicle class.
And here there are certain car specific fields.
Bike specific fields.
Very simple.
No uh extra in that only thing is very simple interface vehicle.

through state design pattern.

--- Composite Pattern (Structural Design Pattern) ---

And today we are going to do a new design pattern which is composite design pattern.

know when, where and how to apply this design pattern.

under this category.

And let's start into the composite design pattern.

So here in this case what composite design pattern says that.
Object inside object.
Right.
What does it means?
Right.
Let's leave this UML for now.
Right.
It's design pattern.
First, let's understand what does object inside object means.
So you need to understand where the composite design pattern comes into the picture whenever object
inside object happens.
For example.
Consider a tree.
Right.
So now consider this tree.

--- Builder Design Pattern (Creational Design Pattern) ---

Hello.
Hey guys.
Welcome to Concept and Coding.
And today I will tell you a very important design pattern which is builder design pattern.
I'll tell you about its, uh, problem.
What is this problem and how we resolve it.
I'll show you the real world solution like.
And we are using it day to day like you might have heard of Stringbuilder, right?
You are using it, right?
So Stringbuilder is based upon totally on the builder design pattern.
And the third and very most another, uh, so many people have confusion regarding it is like builder
versus decorator.
If you see my decorator design pattern video and check out the comment section, you will see many people
have asked that can it be solved through builder or not?
Right.

--- Facade Design Pattern (Structural Design Pattern) ---

Hello.
Hey guys.
Welcome to Concept and Coding.
And today I am going to tell you the very, very important design pattern and also one of my favorite
design pattern.
So why it is favorite because it is very very simple.
Second, it is widely used and I am pretty much sure even though you haven't studied this design pattern,
you might have used this design pattern while coding for sure, right?
So before going this, I will tell you what we will cover today.
So I'll cover facade design pattern.
I will tell you different real world use cases.
Plus at the later part of the video I'll tell you about facade versus proxy pattern.
Also facade versus adapter Pattern because they are little bit similar and many candidates got confused
between this.
Right.
So with I will show you different scenarios where can we can use facade with different different examples,
and I hope it will help you to differentiate.
Okay, where to use facade.
When to use facade okay.
So let's start okay.
Let's start with facade.
So what is when to use it.
When to use and why.
So facade is used when we have to.
Hide the system complexity.
From the client that's all.
So when to use facade.
Just always remember this.
Whenever we have to hide the system complexity from the client, then we have to go for facade.
For example car.
Okay, so we all know that whenever.
So let's say you are a client.
So as a user user we who are driving the car, we are a client.
So user know that when you press or when you press accelerate method, accelerate function, what will
happen?
A speed goes up and when the user press brake what will happen?
Speed goes down right.
So that's how as a client we know the functionality.
If I click if I press accelerate, pedal speed will go up.
If I push, the brake pedal, speed will go down.
But the real complexity, the system complexity behind the accelerate does.
Clients knows it?
No.
So when actual this method got called right.
Maybe internally a system has so many classes which are interacting with each other to actually make
the speed up, right?
So as a client, I do not bother like how actually the speed is getting increased.
Okay.
This engine is interacting with gear.
Gear is interacting with this.
We don't know right?
We we are less bothered about how the different components of a card car interact together.
So that speed goes up.
We don't care.
All we know, all we care about, is if we press the accelerator, pedal speed should go up similarly.
Client doesn't bother about what all components interact with each other so that speed goes down right?
We all care about if we call break, speed should go down.
So if we go with this definition.
So now what we have done is we have to hide the system complexity.
So this is our system right.
And it is very complicated system.
Let's say there are thousands of components in a car which are interacting with each other right.
So we have to hide this complexity from the client.
And we have to hide this from the client.
Client should not know about this thousand components how they are interacting.
Right.
So he's all interested in acceleration brake and other functionality right.
So this is nothing but a facade layer.
If it is not much clear as of now, don't worry, I'll tell you many more examples.
It will get very, very much clear.
But currently only remember that when to use facade.
Whenever we want to hide the system complexity from the client, right?
So with this example, at least you know that there is a system.
Which has many components or many classes.
You can say that many classes.
Class one, class two, class three, class four.
Or let's say class 100 something.
And they are interacting with each other.
So it's a very complex system right.
So facade is just a this layer.
And this is actually the client.
So client interact with facade and facade internally interact with the system.
Right.
So this is what known as facade.
But always remember that we are not forcing client to always talk with facade, right?
Client if wanted.
If client wanted.
Client can also talk directly with system.
Right.
So facade is not a mandatory.
First of all we have to always remember that we should not enforce client to use facade if we if client
wanted to use any of the class.
So let's say client say that okay.
No I want to use class one.
Class two, class three.
So we should not force client to use facade to do that.
So it's client is independent.
Like if I want to use client should be able to use the components independently.
And whatever the facade logic client say that okay I can build that logic into mine.
It should be open.
So but it's client choice that whether they want to build a complex by themself or better way, is that
we can create a facade layer and client can interact with that.
Right?
So all the complexity and logic goes here.
So all client has to do is just call the facade.
So till now it is clear simple.

In logic of this classes.

So better way is bringing a facade layer order facade layer.

We will keep at order facade layer right.

It only impact facade right.

So clients should not even bother about all this logic.

Facade versus proxy.
Now you can ask me here how the facade and proxy are different.
So if you remember in proxy what we have done Let's say we have employee Dao.
So here this is let's say in proxy we have created an interface.
This is an interface.
And this is the real object employee impl implementation.
So in proxy we create one proxy here.
Employee DTO proxy and proxy internally calls this impl.
So client calls proxy.
So client call proxy and proxy actually calls the actual object right.
So client calling employee DTO proxy and employee DTO proxy is calling the this one.
So now you can say that hey it is very much similar to facade here now in facade.
Client is calling facade and facade is calling the actual objects.
Right.
So you can see how they are different.
First difference is if you see the proxy proxy is for a particular object.
So let's say this object.
So whatever the interface of this class proxy is also of the same interface.
Right.
So this is of the same interface.
And proxy takes care of a particular object.
So now here inside it's a employee DTO right.
So proxy will take care of this only proxy.
Let's say if there are another DTO let's say order DTO proxy.
This proxy will not take care of this one.
Right.
So a proxy can take care of only.
So this is wrong.
A proxy can take care of only one object, and the type of this proxy is also same as that object which
it is taking care of.
Now C facade versus adapter.
So in adapter you know right we have an actual interface.
Or you can say that original.
Interface right.
So let's say I take an an example of let's say this socket.
So this is an original interface.
And then you have a client.
So let's say your client is this.
Right.
So they are currently incompatible.
So client and your original interface are incompatible.
Table.
So we create an adapter in mid.
So that client can talk with adapter.
Adapter can talk with original.
And it can do kind of a mediator right.
So you got right adapter is used to bring the compatibility between the client and original interface.
Right.
And the use of facade is to hide the complexity right from the client.
I hope this clarify you the facade design pattern, and you will not get confused with proxy and adapter.
And any time you will get a use case, you should be able to know when to use facade.
Okay guys.
So that's, uh, that's all from my side today and I'll see you soon.
Bye, guys.
Good night.

--- Flyweight Design Pattern (Structural Design Pattern) ---

Hey guys.
Welcome to Concept and Coding.
And this is Shreyansh.
And today we are going to do this flyweight design pattern right.
So I am making it right also okay so now let's go more deeper into this flyweight design pattern.
So two important questions comes on top of this design pattern.
And this one has been recently asked right.
Design a word processor or text editor.

So there are a lot of games.

So let's first start with its definition like what is this definition says.
So first of all it's a structural design pattern right.
And what it helps to reduce the memory usage.
This pattern helps to reduce the memory usage by sharing data among multiple objects.
So you you should get one hint that whenever interviewer says that, uh, hey, we need to reduce the
our memory is very limited.
So it should strike in your mind that it can it be solved through flyweight design pattern or not.

And creation of object is expensive.
Then it's another hint.
So I also wanted to tell you about this two words, which you will, uh, see a lot in this, uh, pattern.
One is intrinsic data and extrinsic data.
Generally I say is Andrew or Barry.
So I would say that what intrinsic data is, the data which is shared among objects are called instancing
data.
So here in the robots I have created five lakh of robots of humans.
So all five lakhs are sharing this two type and body.
So this is known as your intrinsic data right.
Extrinsic data is changes based on the client input and differ from one object to another.
So here if you see that the client is putting certain value right for the x axis y axis.
So it so extrinsic data is changed based on the client input.
So now here if you see that my x axis and y axis change based on the client input where I want to showcase
the robot.
So this is your extrinsic data.
And two robots cannot share this.
So two robots have different x axis y axis.
So they cannot share this data.
So all the data which cannot be shared between two objects and its value coming from the client, those
are known as extrinsic data.
All the fields.
Or you can say that all the data which is shared among the different objects, that is known as instancing
data.
Okay.
So just understand this.
We're gonna use this term okay.
So now you know that in what scenarios we have to use this pattern.
Now let's see how to solve the issue in the gaming scenario.
How to solve the issue.
So first let's go through these four steps.
And then we'll uh see the demo.
So from object remove all the extrinsic data and keep only instancing data.
So what first step says that remove from this whatever the object which you are creating remove all
the extrinsic data from it.
So here extracting data is these two.
So remove this remove this remove all the extrinsic data and keep only instancing data.
Okay.
And after that, this object is called as flyweight object.
So now this objects generally which has only instancing data means data which is shared among different
objects.
These kind of objects is known as flyweight object generally.
Okay.
So the first step is remove all the data from the object.
Then this class can be immutable.
Immutable means.
You can once it once you have uh give the value to this field, you cannot change it.

You can make those private and only provide the getter no setter methods when there is no setter method.

you won't be able to change its value.
So they are immutable.
Okay, so second thing is, since we are saying that these are the common data, these are the interesting
data which are shared among multiple objects.
So we can make them immutable so that once the value is defined it cannot be changed again.

What.

--- Iterator Design Pattern (Behavioral Design Pattern) ---

Hey guys.
Welcome to Concept Encoding.
And this is Shreyansh.
And today we are going to do a iterator pattern okay.
So let's start.
So before we jump into how iterator pattern works let's first understand what is the usage of the iterator
pattern.
And to understand the usage of iterator pattern there is no better example than Java collection itself.
So this is the diagram which I have taken from my Java playlist, right where I have explained the collection
in depth.
So here, if you see that in the Java collections there are different types of collection like priority
queue, array, dequeue, linked list ArrayList stack Hashset linked Hashset.
Right.
Each stores the data in their particular manner like linked list uses which data structure linked list
ArrayList using which data structure arrays.
Right?
So each maintains the collect the data in their own manner, right?
But whenever you want to iterate over it?
Do you client does have to know that okay this is linked list.
I have to access in this particular manner.
No okay.
This is stack.
I need to access the element in this particular manner.
Does client bother about the knowledge of how to access the element?
No.
So for this collection has provided one iterator method.
So here in this example I have created a new linked hash set.
This and I have stored certain data.
Now it might used uh hash HashMap internally.
Right.
Uh on HashMap plus linked list internally to store the data, whatever it would be.
Right.
But does client.
So this is let's say you are this is your client.
Does client has to have this knowledge about it.
Like okay if I want to iterate I am accessing linked hash set.
So it should be accessed in this manner?
No.
So client doesn't bother about what type of collection it is, right.
How the data is stored in this particular collection.
It knows that the collection has given me this iterator method.
Once I get this iterator object, I can use Hasnext and next and it will help me to iterate.
Hasnext will tell that okay, is there any more element?
If yes, go inside and get me the value out of it.
Okay, so now no matter if I'm using linked hash set here or Priorityqueue here or stack here, this
logic will remain the same.
This will work for all.
Okay, but you can ask me a question, right?
Hey, how this actually works, right?
How this iterator which collections expose this iterator method, how this actually works.
Actually that this iterator pattern.
So if you understand the iterator pattern, you will get your answer that how this code works for all
type of collections.
Okay.
So now let's go into deeper.
It is a behavioral design pattern okay.
That provide a way to access element of a collection sequentially.
So that is the first use.
It helps you to access element of a collection sequentially without exposing the underlying representation
of the collection.
You don't have to worry about what is the underlying data structure used to store the data.
You can it iterator pattern can help you to access the element sequentially, right?
You don't have to worry about the underlying representation of the collection.
But how?

And next.

Is that okay.

to okay.

--- Mediator Design Pattern (Behavioral Design Pattern) ---

Hey guys, welcome to the new LED video where we are going to design an online auction system using
mediator design pattern.
So with that today I will also make this mediator pattern tick Mark.
Right.
So and also one more thing.
While I will tell you about the mediator pattern and the online auction system.
So you can say that this airline management system this can also you can say that is an example of mediator
design pattern.
So have that in mind also.
Right.
So we will go with that okay.
So let's start designing the online auction system.
But before let's understand what is mediator design pattern.
Right.
And using that we will design the online auction system.
The mediator pattern is a behavioral design pattern okay.
So it's a behavioral pattern.
It encourages loose coupling by keeping objects from referring to each other explicitly, and allow
them to communicate through a mediator object.
Now, what does it mean that whenever two object has to talk with each other, right?
Instead of directly talking to each other, they will talk with some mediator, right?
That's the name.
So they will send a message to mediator.
And mediator will pass the message to the receiver.
And similarly, if any message comes from receiver it goes to the mediator and mediator pass to it.
So they don't know about each other.
This object one and object two doesn't know about each other.
And whenever they have to pass the information to any other object, they will pass it to the mediator.
That's what it says, that it brings a loose coupling by keeping objects from referring to each other
explicitly.
So no two objects Explicitly call each other right.
So and allow them to communicate through a mediator object.
So they both are communicating through mediator object.
So that's what its definition is.
And this is what its UML is.
So I will come to the UML later.
Now lets see its online auction system example.
And we will construct this UML itself using the example.
And then we'll come to see it would be very very easy for you to grasp.
So you know that what happens in the online auction system is in the online auction system.
There are so many bidders, right?
So they are bidders and they bid, they bid, they place bid.
Let's say this person has placed a bid for ₹1,000.
Now, whenever a person place a bid, other person knows that, okay, there is a bid which has been
placed for ₹1,000.
Now they have an option whether to increase it or whether to place a bet with a higher amount or not.
Right.
So now they can other person can place a bet with higher amount, right?
And whoever keep the higher amount bid that wins.
So that's generally an auction system.
And now let's see that in this auction system this person's let's consider them as consider them as
an object.
They don't have to directly communicate with each other.
Right.
Even though they are placing a bid, there is you can say that a mediator like they send their bid to
this and this will tell that, okay, this is the bid amount.
Anybody want to raise the bid or not?
So they are not directly talking to each other.
They are talking through a mediator.
Right.
So that's why I told you that online auction system is one of the example of mediator design pattern.
And similarly airline management system I also find that this airline management system is also very
good example of mediator design pattern where multiple flights right where multiple flights interact

Very simple class right?

in future, if like no matter 50 or 100, whatever the operations you are adding, keep on adding it.

--- MVC Design Pattern ---

Hey guys.
Welcome to concept encoding.
This is Shreyansh.
So I have got a message from an engineer that in an LD round the question has been asked is MVC design
pattern.
And that's where he got a little bit not able to answer that.
So today I'm going to cover the MVC design pattern.
But here I would tell you that we have solved so many question like snake and ladder, parking lot,
elevator split wise bookmyshow, etc. in all we have used this MVC right?
But generally I considered MVC as an architecture rather than a design pattern.
That's where I haven't specifically mentioned that this is the design pattern, right?
But still, if an interview interviewer asked you that MVC design pattern or let's say design snake
and ladder using MVC, so don't get confused that hey, we have solved Design Snake and later in LD.
But now what does it mean using MVC?
Okay, so I'll tell you what actually MVC is.
And then we don't have to worry.
Don't worry much about this.
So MVC contains three parts.
First is model.
Second is your view and third is controller.
So model view controller.
That's what MVC stands for.
So all the latest framework like spring boot in Java that follows MVC.
Similarly for Python I think the major framework is Django right.
So that also follows MVC.
So this is very popular for certain reason I will tell you the advantage and disadvantage of it.
But first understand what is it.
Okay.
So now here if you see that how this three Parts connect to each other, and this is generally an architecture
of your application.
How your application should look like.
Okay so here first we have view right.
So here let's make it like this.
You have view then you have controller.
Then you have model.
Okay.
So view interact with controller.
Controller interacts with model.
Through model you can interact with your DB.
And your clients generally interact with your view.
Okay.
So generally this is how your whole application architecture looks like.
So this is generally your front end application.
Front end app.
Right.
So all the front end like button form where user can enter or give command right.
So it can do with the view.

So in an so this pattern is used.
Or you can say that this pattern is specifically meant to store the object history.

--- Template Method Design Pattern (Behavioral Design Pattern) ---

Hey guys.
Welcome to Concept Encoding.
And this is Shreyansh.
And in the low level design today we are going to see template method pattern.
So I'm taking it right.
So only one pattern would be left after this which is interpreter pattern okay.
So let's start with our latest topic which is template method design pattern.
So here the category which this pattern belongs to is the behavioral.
And you need to understand why it's required and when to use.
And before I start I will tell you one point that we use this pattern a lot.
And if you are working you might not know that you are using this pattern.
But this is very frequently used pattern in the industry.
Okay.
So let's read this and then I will tell you that how do you identify that I have to use this pattern
when you want all classes to follow the specific steps to process the task, but also you need to provide
the flexibility that each class can have their own logic in that specific steps.
What does that mean?
Let's take an example.
Understand with an example.
Let's.
Let me take an a payments uh example.
So you know that there are two, uh, type of payments.
One is you when you are making payments to your friend.
Right.
So let's say it is paid to friend flow.
And there is another is when you purchase something online.
Right.
So when you purchase something online okay.
So this is let's say pay to merchant flow.
So let's say in the payment there are two flows when you pay to your friend.
When you pay to a merchant.
Now, no matter whether you are paying to your friend or whether you are paying to a merchant, you
want all the flows to follow a particular steps.
So Payment steps which you want to follow is that say validate, validate request after second they
will debit the money.
Third should be calculate.
Let's say I'm just taking an example.
Calculate fees for the platform.
And the fourth is credit money.
Okay.
So validate if your request whatever it is request is correct or not.
After yes everything is right then debit the money from this user.
Charge certain fee.
The platform should charge certain fee.
That's how they will earn and then credit the money to the receiver.
Either your friend or either to the merchant.
So no matter whether you are paying to a friend or whether you are paying to the merchant, what you
want is you want these flows to follow this specific steps in an order.
Now how you will do it generally let's say that.
So now let's say that I have this payment interface payment, which is an interface.
And let's say you have send money method.
Right.
So here payment I would say that let's say this is the method name.
Task name is send money.
So now your one child would be which one pay two friend.
This would be paid to send flow.
Another child would be.
Pay to merchant flow.
Now they have to override this.
Send money.
Right?
So they will override this.
Send money and they provide their own implementation.
It will also override this.
Send money and provide its implementation.
Now we want that okay.
For this send money.
All this classes should follow this specific steps validate, then debit, then calculate fees and then
credit money.
So it can do first validate and then debit and then compute fees and then credit.
But it is possible that this might not follow this.
It will do validate.
It will do.
First let's say credit and then do debit and then compute fees.
Who knows.
Right.
So but what I'm trying to say is that our requirement is when you want all classes to follow the specific
steps to process the task.
Right.
Then first, this is your first hint when you want all classes to follow a specific step to process
the task.
So what is the task here is send money.
I want to send the money, but I want all classes here.
All the child classes of this payment, no matter if any future, any more flows comes.
If anybody is doing send money, they need to process a specific steps.
What step this is steps right.
So that is the first hint.
Second hint is that I also need to provide the flexibility that each class can have their own specific
logic and specific step.
So validate request might be pay to friend flow might be doing validation certain differently.
This might be doing certain differently.

It do not charge any fees 0% fees.

Your payment platform get earns.
So what I'm trying to say is that each flow can have different logic for this step.
Okay, so you got it.
How you will identify whether you have to use template pattern.
When all classes write all child classes, you want them to follow a specific step to process the task,
and also flexibility that each class can have their own logic in that specific steps.
Because here if you see that send money right, they are overriding it.
Right.
And they will provide the send money capability.
But it might be possible that it might not follow the specific steps.
So they are following some sequence.
They are following some sequence.
But who will guarantee that they are following the same sequence?
That's where the template method design pattern comes into the picture.
So now see this how you can do that.

--- Interpreter Pattern (Behavioral Design Pattern) ---

Hey guys.
Welcome to Concept Encoding.
And this is Shreyansh.
And in LD today we are going to do the last design pattern which is interpreted design pattern okay.
So let's start it without wasting any time.
This design pattern belongs to which pattern category behavioral pattern it belongs to behavioral pattern
okay.
So now let's understand first what does interpreter pattern means.
Consider this a hand sign.
What do you interpret out of it.
So either you can interpret it as a stop or somebody saying hi to you.
Or it might be the notion of number five.
Or you can say that it might be like a raising and hand raising a hand.
So this sign can be interpreted in different ways.
So it depends upon context in what context We are providing or what is the context then based on that
context, we can say that, hey, it means a stop.
For example, context.
I give you that it's a traffic signal or something, then stop sign there might be like stop somewhere.

--- Object Pool Design Pattern (Creational Design Pattern) ---

Hey guys.
Welcome to Concept and Coding.
This is Shreyansh.
And today we are going to cover a new topic in low level design which is object pool design pattern.
So here if you see I actually I don't have any intention to cover this topic, but it is seems like
this question is getting asked a lot in interviews.
I am getting a lot of comments to cover it up.
Okay, so let's quickly see it because this is very, very simple.
But I think I found one thing where engineers are making a mistake in this very simple design pattern.
So I will touch that point also.
So the category which object pool design pattern belongs to is Creational design pattern.
What does it says?
It manages the pool of reusable objects like Dbconnection objects.
If you are familiar with Java, you already know thread pool in which there is a pool of threads.
And whenever you need a particular, whenever you need a particular thread you take out from the pool,
use it.
And once you use it, you add add it back.
Okay, so on the similar concept, this object pool design pattern is present.
So it manages the pool of reusable objects like DB connection which can be reused.
Okay.
It borrows from the pool, use it and then return it back to the pool.
So this is a two line definition which I can come up with for an object pool design pattern.
Advantage and disadvantage I will come to later.
Once we understand what exactly it is, then we will come to this point quickly.
So here we have three points 123.
So object pool design pattern.
So we have a pool of what objects.
So in this sense these objects are nothing but resource.
Okay.
So resource is the nothing but an object which need to be managed okay.
So that is the first is your resource.
You should have a resource.
Second thing is resource pool manager.
So now let's say you have multiple resources.
There should be some manager which manage those pool.
So we have second is resource pool manager okay.
So resource pool manager I told you it borrows from the pool.
Use it and return it back to the pool.
So this pool manager handles that capability.
So it maintains a list of free resources which you can use it.
It also maintain In what all the resources which are currently in use once they finish their work from
in use, it moved back to the free list.
Okay, so free list plus.
Free list plus in use is your total number of resource in a pool.
Okay.
Then you have something called initial pool size.
How in a initially in a pool how many resources are available and what is the maximum pool size?
How Max?
What is the maximum number of objects we can created in a pool?
Okay, so these are the member variables which is present into resource pool manager.
And these are the two methods get resource.
So whenever any one like say any client which is this third point.
Whenever any client want a particular resource it requests a resourceful manager.
Get me the resource and what it will do.
Resource pool manager.
It will check its pool, which is free, and it says that hey, if there is any free resource present
in this list, it will assign it and put that into in use.
And once client is done, it will call release resource right.
That particular object is removed from in use and move it back to the free list.
Okay.
So client is client has a relationship with resource pool manager.
Resource pool manager has a relationship with the resource okay.
So this looks very simple right.
And we can easily code it.

But it is very very simple object pool design pattern.
But there is one problem in this.
I hope you should have identified this.
If you don't able to identify that and if you code like this, definitely I can say that the interviewer
will pinpoint that one.
The thing is, the object pool design pattern is used with singleton design pattern and required thread
safety while acquiring and releasing the resource.
Why?
So here if you see that if your this class is not singleton class, then your client will create new
objects.
So what if I will create another new DB connection pool manager?
Now I created two times.
So when I created two times objects.
So ultimately you now have two objects where you have created free connection three here, three here.
If I create another new it will create new object which has three more.
Right.
So this breaks the purpose for which we are trying to solve.
So that's why we say that object pool design pattern is always used with singleton design pattern and
thread safety is must if you are designing it.
So singleton, you already know that we have already covered this.
So DB connection pool.
So here if you see I have now changed it to a singleton.
So the object for this one I have put private static right.
So private is it's only be accessible through this class not through outside.
Static is it belongs to this class not any object.
And currently instance is null.
So if client Ain't want to get an object of this class.
So I have exposed one method get instance right.
So this is the Getinstance method I have make public and static because you can't create an object of
it.
Constructor is private, constructor is private, so you can't create an object of it.
So that's why I have called the make this method static so that you can call it from a class.
So now this is double locking I am using it.
So I'm not explaining this.
You might already know it through singleton design pattern.
So now this will make sure that only one object is created for this class.
Okay.
So this client, no matter how many times it will call Getinstance get instance, only one object would
have been created.
And when you would have created one object, it would have created a pool with just three.
Okay.
And so this is the singleton part.
And the thread safety part is whenever you are doing get connection release connection right.
That required thread safety.
So means if multiple threads are trying to get a connection or releasing the connection, this list
should not get corrupted, right?
Only one thread at a time should update it.
So that's why the get db connection I have put synchronized here, right?
You can also use locking.
So in Java uh playlist you will see the locking videos.
But I have for simplicity I've just put a synchronize in the method itself.
So it will put a lock internally.
And only one thread can go inside this method.
And rest of the thing is same, right?
And similarly in the release db connection to maintain a thread safety I have put a synchronized here.
Okay.
So this point is very very important if you are designing an object pool design pattern.
Without this, if you have just simply coded it in a hurry, I would 100% say that the interviewer will
take the interview in wrong direction, because this is the crux for this object pool design pattern.
Now let's come to the advantage and disadvantage.
So very very visible advantages.
It reduces the overhead of creating and destroying the frequently required object.
Because now let's say that Dbconnection is object is very very resource intensive object.
It creates it takes a let's say a lot of time and it takes a lot of time also.
Right.
It's a resource intensive object.
So if you have already created beforehand certain you are maintaining the pool, you are actually reducing
the overhead of creating it, destroying it.
right and also helps in improving the latency.
So it reduces the latency also and reduces the overhead of creating and destroying frequently required
object.
Okay.
Also is prevent resource exhaustion by managing the number of resource intensive object creation.
So now let's say that if I am creating one object of this which takes, let's say ten MB, just for
example, if there is no control how many objects you can create of it, and if you create a large amount
of objects, you can go out of resource exhaustion, memory exhaustion.
So you can control it through pool that okay, only 50 objects can be created.
Only 20 objects can be created.
Whatever.
It's just an example.
So it prevent resource exhaustion by managing the number of resources.
So these are the top three advantages comes to my mind when I think about object pool design pattern.
But there are certain disadvantages also.
One very, very big disadvantage is resource leakage can happen if object is not handled properly and
not being returned to the pool back, its lost right?
Some leakage will happen.
That object would still be active and it's not destroyed.
But you can't access it.
You.
It's a resource leakage happen.
It didn't add.
Add it to the pool back.
Right.
You require more memory because of managing the pool.
So now this DB connection pool manager class will also take some memory also.
Right.
So that is your needed some extra memory right.
So that is also one disadvantage.
Pool management required thread safety which is additional overhead.
I already told you it it needed singleton plus thread safety.
So that puts an additional overhead.
You have to make sure that your the get the get resource and release resource should be thread safe
adds application complexity because of managing the pool.
So this adds certain complexity.
Also if in a big application.
Definitely the logic inside this also would be pretty complicated also.
So it brings application complexity.
So this is something I wanted to cover.
Object pool design pattern I hope that clarifies it.
It's a very, very simple design pattern.
But don't use it without thread safety and singleton without that it is totally incomplete.
Okay, I hope this will help you in your interview and if you have any doubts, please feel free to
comment me in the comment section.

Now what is polling.
So inside a polling what will happen is.
So you have a client.
And there is a server.
So client will ask is there a message.
Is there a message for me.
Server will say no.
And then connection closed right.
So you know right HTTP maintain a TCP connection.
So every time client asking something from server it first do a handshake.
shake, uh, do a TCP connection and then ask.
So every time after every response, the connection closed in the polling again.
After some time again message client will ask is message is present for me?
Client will say no, still no messages.
And then again connection break.
So now here if you see in the polling client is again and again asking the question to server.
And most of the time answer is no only.
But maybe sometime let's say yes, you have a message and it will send.
But if you see there is a lot of wastage of resources, connection again how many connection you will
maintain, right?
And so many times you have to create a connection, then close the connection.
So this is unnecessary kind of a load on a server.
So polling is not generally scalable right.
So that's why we don't go for polling.
Now there is a little bit enhancement over polling and what is known as long polling.
So let's understand about long polling or what is also known as pushing.
So here again we have a client.
We have a server.
So again client maintain a HTTP connection.
And it asks is there a message.
Now server will not response it.
Server will wait for particular threshold.
Let's say threshold is one minute.
So thread.
